questions.question: What does the Baudhāyana-Pythagoras theorem state?
questions.correct_answer: In a right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides
questions.explanation: This relationship is written as a² + b² = c².

questions.question: What is the hypotenuse of a right triangle?
questions.correct_answer: The side opposite the right angle
questions.explanation: It is the longest side of the triangle.

questions.question: What type of triangle satisfies the Pythagoras theorem?
questions.explanation: The theorem applies only when one angle is 90°.

questions.question: What is the formula for the diagonal of a square?
questions.explanation: It is derived using the Pythagoras theorem.

questions.question: What is a Pythagorean triple?
questions.correct_answer: Three integers that satisfy a² + b² = c²
questions.explanation: They represent the sides of a right triangle.

questions.question: Give an example of a Pythagorean triple.
What is a primitive Pythagorean triple?
questions.correct_answer: A triple with no common factor other than 1

questions.question: What is a scaled Pythagorean triple?
questions.correct_answer: A triple obtained by multiplying a primitive triple by the same integer

questions.question: How can you check if a triangle is right-angled?
questions.correct_answer: Check if the square of the longest side equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides

questions.question: What is the diagonal of a unit square?
questions.explanation: It comes from applying the Pythagoras theorem to a square with side 1.

notes.title: Baudhāyana-Pythagoras Theorem
notes.content: It relates the three sides of a right triangle using the equation a² + b² = c².

notes.title: Right Triangle
notes.content: A triangle with one angle equal to 90°.

notes.title: Pythagorean Triples
notes.content: Integer sets such as (3,4,5) that satisfy the Pythagoras relation.

notes.title: Diagonal of Square
notes.content: The diagonal of a square with side a equals a√2.

notes.title: Applications
notes.content: The theorem helps calculate distances, diagonals, and heights in geometry and real-life problems.

facts.fact: The Baudhāyana-Pythagoras theorem was known in ancient Indian mathematics before Pythagoras.
The diagonal of a square always forms two right triangles.
The Pythagoras theorem is widely used in geometry, construction, and navigation.
